commit.message:
docs: design doc for same-type query revalidation [#983] (#998)

Design doc for MutationEventBus-based same-type query revalidation.
After adversarial review (DX, product/scope, technical feasibility),
the design was simplified from cross-entity invalidation to same-type
only. Cross-entity reactivity deferred to #993 (deep normalization).

Key decisions:
- Same-type only: mutation on tasks → revalidate tasks queries
- Always emit for all mutation kinds (filtered list correctness)
- No invalidation map or codegen needed
- Opt-out via skipInvalidation on MutationMeta
- Bus is payload-free: emit(entityType) only
- 3 implementation phases (bus, handler, query subscription)

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix/restore workspace deps (#103)

* feat: add normalized chat feed and executor seam

* feat: Rust backend + Bun dashboard rewrite

Rust backend (43 files, 4,086 lines):
- 7 crates: core, db, server, executors, watcher, git, cli
- Board parser: Obsidian Kanban markdown with roundtrip fidelity
- Dispatcher: auto-dispatch from Ready column with agent tags
- Spawn limiter: 5 global + 2 per-project concurrency
- 9 executors: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Amp, Cursor, OpenCode, Droid, Qwen, Copilot
- SQLite (WAL), SSE events, webhook HMAC-SHA256
- 6.7MB binary, 195ms startup

Bun dashboard (10 files, 505 lines):
- React 19 + Tailwind v4 + Vite
- Real-time status, sessions, projects, executors, event feed

10 tests passing.

* fix: remove duplicate dashboard, keep existing packages/web

* perf: switch runtime from Node.js to Bun

Benchmarks (same codebase, just runtime swap):
- CLI startup (co --help): 66ms vs 147ms (2.2x faster)
- co doctor: 106ms vs 325ms (3x faster)
- Tests: 194ms/43 tests vs 808ms/22 tests (4x faster, finds more tests)
- Core build: 625ms vs 865ms (1.4x faster)
- Dashboard ready: 113ms

Changes:
- Shebang: #!/usr/bin/env bun (was node)
- LaunchAgent: uses bun binary path
- Fix type errors: DashboardRole 'editor' -> 'operator', null vs undefined
- Fix react-markdown v10 incompatibility (downpin to v9)
- Add missing remark-gfm dependency
- Add ChatPanelProps.agentName

* chore: add .playwright-cli to gitignore and commit Cargo.lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: replace pnpm lockfile with bun lockfile

* refactor: extract state.rs into focused service modules

Split the 955-line state.rs monolith into:
- state/types.rs: data types and constants
- state/session_manager.rs: session lifecycle
- state/session_store.rs: persistence
- state/workspace.rs: workspace/worktree ops
- state/helpers.rs: utility functions
- state/mod.rs: AppState core + re-exports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move files/diff/checks handlers from sessions.rs (~300 lines) into
session_workspace.rs for better separation of concerns.

- Stop polling when session reaches terminal state
  (done, killed, errored, terminated, merged, cleanup)

* test: add unit tests for config, task, types, and workspace

- config: defaults, effective_port, preferences
- task: state transitions, cancel, retry, display
- types: AgentKind parse/display/serialization, Priority, HealthGrade
- workspace: path resolution, join_script

* fix: resolve TypeScript null narrowing in branch loader closure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add multipart feature flag to axum, update mono-repo build scripts,
and exclude user workspace data directory from version control.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Rust backend route modules for all API endpoints

Extract route handlers into dedicated modules: attachments, auth,
boards, context-files, filesystem, github, notifications, repositories,
and workspaces. Includes timing-safe auth token comparison, cookie
SameSite hardening, broadcast lag recovery with synthetic refresh
events, and proper HTTP status codes on config update errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden CORS policy with explicit headers and configurable origins

Replace allow_headers(Any) with explicit CONTENT_TYPE, AUTHORIZATION,
ACCEPT allowlist. Add cors_origins config field to ServerConfig for
user-defined extra origins. Make AppState::new async for tokio-based
session loading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve TOCTOU races and concurrency issues in session management

- Move is_live check after sessions write lock in append_and_apply
- Reorder send_to_session to persist before sending input
- Use drain() instead of full buffer copy in append_output
- Use safe session_id slicing with get().unwrap_or()
- Replace can_spawn with atomic acquire in drain_ready
- Add child.wait() after kill() to prevent zombie processes
- Cap merge_assistant_fragment contains check to last 512 bytes
- Make load_sessions_from_disk fully async with tokio::fs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: update state layer, executors, and watcher for async I/O

Async AppState init, executor process management improvements including
zombie process reaping, PTY poll interval tuning (500ms), and
tokio::fs migration in file watcher. Update agent executor
implementations and CLI entry point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: migrate frontend API layer to Rust backend proxy

Remove all 31 Next.js API route handlers and route requests directly
to the Rust backend via proxy.ts. Keeps only the agents route for
binary path validation with isSafeBinary using path.basename.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace frontend service layer with shared utilities

Remove auth.ts, event-bus-singleton.ts, executionBackend.ts, and
services.ts (superseded by Rust backend). Add agentUtils.ts with
shared formatCurrentModelLabel and normalizeAgentName. Add liveEvents.ts
for SSE snapshot subscription. Fix getAttentionLevel errored status
contradiction and parseCost zero-value handling with nullish coalescing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve polling leaks and SSR issues in frontend hooks

- useSession: move fallback timeout to useRef, fix stale notFoundRef
  by using [id] dependency
- useSessionOutputStream: add mountedRef guard in SSE error handler
- useAgents: replace window.setTimeout with globalThis for SSR safety
- useSessionFeed: increase hidden poll interval to 5s, refactor
  refreshLoop from labeled while(true) to do/while
- useSessions: align DashboardSession type with Session interface

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve frontend components with scroll, rendering, and UX fixes

- ChatPanel: fix scroll-to-bottom to only fire on new entries, add
  rich tool call rendering with review findings blocks, remove static
  SetupScriptHint, fix slash command key to use command only
- SessionDetail: allow overview tab in resolveSessionTab
- SessionOverview: add try/catch to clipboard.writeText, fix parseCost

- SessionDiff: remove no-op parsedFiles useCallback
- WorkspaceKanban: cap task rendering at 50 per column with overflow

- Sidebar: surface archive failure with red icon + tooltip
- Add AbortController timeouts on handleSend/handleInterrupt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update pages for Rust backend integration and type safety

Fix LinkedBoardResponse type narrowing in page.tsx, remove unsafe
double cast in session type alignment, and add anti-FOUT comment
to layout.tsx theme script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): migrate CI and release workflows from pnpm to bun

- CI: split into parallel Rust (check, clippy, test) and Frontend
  (typecheck, build) jobs using bun + dtolnay/rust-toolchain
- Release: replace pnpm with bun install, add Rust build/test steps,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): migrate security and codeql workflows from pnpm to bun

Replace pnpm/action-setup with oven-sh/setup-bun in security audit

(v4 and v3 respectively).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): build workspace deps in dependency order before typecheck

Core and plugins must be built before CLI typecheck can resolve
workspace:* imports. Reorder CI to: build (core → plugins → cli → web)
then typecheck, instead of typecheck-first which fails on missing
dist/ directories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: auto-release on merge to main with conventional commit versioning

- Trigger release on push to main (replaces manual workflow_dispatch-only)
- Auto-detect version bump from conventional commits (feat→minor, fix→patch, feat!→major)
- Keep workflow_dispatch as manual override for explicit version type
- Skip bot release commits to prevent infinite loops
- Only create tags (no push to protected main branch)
- Add idempotency: skip if tag or npm version already exists
- Set cancel-in-progress: false to prevent interrupted releases
- Add required CI status checks to branch protection (Rust + Frontend)

commit.message:
feat: zero-config UX — inline autoconfig, smart defaults, input validation

run_parallel_cv.sh now works with just 4 args (model_type, ref, reads,
output_vcf). No env vars, no --num_shards needed.

- Inline CPU autoconfig: detect implementer/part, set OneDNN/BF16/jemalloc
- OneDNN always overridden (Docker entrypoint sets it unconditionally)
- Default --num_shards to $(nproc)
- Default --nocompress (uncompressed TFRecords, ~4% faster ME)
- Prefer INT8 ONNX model over FP32 when model_int8_static.onnx exists
- Validate --ref and --reads exist before launching make_examples
- Progress bar (--bar) when tty available, --line-buffer otherwise
- README Quick Start simplified to 6-line docker run

Tested on Hetzner CAX41 (16 vCPU Neoverse-N1, 30 GB):
  Config: cpu=neoverse-n1 bf16=false onednn=0 jemalloc=yes
  INT8 model auto-selected, input validation works, pipeline starts OK.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix public bio page /c/:handle for creators with handles in auth metadata

Creator handles set during onboarding are stored in auth.users
user_metadata, not in directory_members. The bio page only queried
directory_members, so handles like "johnny-rocket" returned 404.

Added /api/resolve-handle serverless endpoint that looks up auth users
by handle via the service role key, returning only public bio fields.
Updated resolveCreator() to call this as a third fallback.

fix: preserve member data across reset commands

When user sends 'merhaba' (reset command), the session data was being
wiped with data: {}, destroying the member lookup results (uyeId, isim,
soyisim, mail) that were just fetched. Now member data is preserved
across reset commands so personalized greetings work.

Also added debug logging to showWelcome to track member data state.

fix: resolve HIGH npm security vulnerabilities via npm audit fix

Eliminates 2 HIGH severity vulnerabilities:
- immutable: Prototype Pollution (GHSA-wf6x-7x77-mvgw)
- minimatch: ReDoS via repeated wildcards (multiple CVEs)

Remaining (9 total, 6 low / 3 moderate) deferred:
- dompurify / fast-xml-parser: version constraint blocks auto-fix (ISSUE-2180)
- @tootallnate/once: jest test dep, needs --force (low risk)
- next@15.5.12: pinned to 15.4.11 (admin 500 fix) — upgrade needs E2E test first

Related: ISSUE-2180, IDEA-606

Split handler monolith, unify macro summation, improve domain naming

- Split main.go (715 lines) into 6 handler files by domain: overview,
  meal, template, food, scan, auth. main.go now ~135 lines (routing,
  middleware, static assets).
- Rename joinID → itemID in routes, handlers, and DB params
- Rename isTemplate → isSavedMeal to match UI terminology
- Unify macro summation: delete view.sumMealItems, add db.SumMealItemMacros,
  pass pre-computed db.Macro to MealTotals/MealTotalsOOB templates
- Extract inline CSS from Header() templ into css/app.css
- Add log.Printf to DB functions that silently return nil on error
  (queryMealSummaries, FoodSearch, GetUserTargets)

from old contract key to new key. The room owner doesn't need to be
online or take special action.

  even when no forms are completed yet

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove maxEncodeWidth/Height that broke AV1 P-frame decode

Setting maxEncodeWidth=3840/maxEncodeHeight=2160 at NVENC init caused
the AV1 sequence header to advertise 3840x2160 max dimensions even for
small frames (e.g. 300x300). Chrome decoded the initial keyframe but
silently failed on subsequent P-frames, resulting in a frozen image
with fps=0 in the viewer.

Remove the hardcoded max dimensions. The default (0 = match encode
size) produces correct AV1 sequence headers. Reconfigure for upsizing
will need full encoder rebuild instead, but downsizing works fine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Steward v1.0.0 — autonomous agent engine (165 tests)

Phase 6: Configuration + retry + production polish
- StewardConfig: YAML-based project settings (.steward/config.yaml)
- Retry with exponential backoff for transient LLM errors (timeout, 429, 503)
- Non-transient errors immediately failover to next provider
- Config merges with explicit constructor args (explicit wins)

Full v1.0.0 capability matrix:
- Async OODA agent loop with parallel tool execution
- 6 tools: bash, read_file, write_file, glob, edit_file, grep
- 4 LLM providers: Google, Mistral, DeepSeek, Anthropic (prana-ordered)
- O(1) Lotus routing (MahaAttention), Iron Dome safety guard
- LLM-based summarization at 70% context budget
- PersistentMemory (JSON-backed, Phoenix atomic writes)
- Project instructions (.steward/instructions.md, CLAUDE.md fallback)
- Git status/diff context in system prompt
- EventBus + SignalBus dual emission
- CLI: steward "task", interactive REPL, --resume
- YAML configuration with sensible defaults

commit.message:
fix T3 Venus similarity: cluster by sign group, fix degree=30 overflow

- reconstructLongitude: clamp degree to 29.99 so Venus at sign boundary
  (stored as degree=30) doesn't overflow into the next sign's tier
- T3 formula: use nearest-boundary distance to artist's sign as primary
  score so all artists within the same compatible sign cluster together
  (all Virgo 57-58%, all Pisces 55-56%, all Libra 52-53%) instead of
  interleaving by raw angular distance
- move angularDist before venusSimilarity (used by T3 nearBound calc)

Exposure dome: full scan radius, height scale slider, smooth interpolation v0.43.0

Dome now extends to the actual scan radius (1-30km) instead of a symbolic 250m.
Height scale slider (1-50%, default 5%) controls dome height relative to radius.
Changing height/opacity rebuilds dome live without re-fetching from backend.
120 azimuth wedges with linear interpolation between the 720 ray samples for
smooth transitions. Adaptive ring count (12-25) based on scan radius.

commit.message:
perf(compiler): skip effect wrapping for static JSX expressions (#1007)

* fix(compiler): classify destructured props as reactive sources in JsxAnalyzer [#1006]

Destructured prop bindings (e.g., title from { title }: Props) are
getter-backed (__props.xxx) after the PropsDestructuringTransformer.
Reading them inside an effect tracks signal dependencies; reading them
outside does not. The JsxAnalyzer was missing these bindings from its
reactiveSourceVars set because they are function parameters, not
VariableInfo entries.

This also fixes a pre-existing bug where .map() on prop-backed arrays
skipped __list() reconciliation because the expression was classified
as non-reactive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(compiler): skip __attr for static attribute expressions [#1006]

Thread JsxExpressionInfo to processAttribute() and check the reactive
flag. Non-literal, non-reactive attribute expressions now emit the
guarded setAttribute pattern instead of __attr() — eliminating a
domEffect allocation per static attribute.

The guarded pattern correctly handles boolean HTML attributes
(disabled={false} removes the attribute, disabled={true} sets empty
string) matching __attr()'s runtime behavior.

Reactive attributes (props, signal-api properties, computed values)
continue to use __attr() for effect tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(compiler): skip __child for static child expressions [#1006]

Static non-literal child expressions (e.g., const labels, imports, utility
calls on static args) now emit __insert() instead of __child(() => ...),
eliminating unnecessary domEffect() allocations. Reactive expressions
(signals, computed, props, reactive sources) continue to use __child.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(compiler): Phase 3 — hydration validation and edge case coverage [#1006]

- Add hydration test verifying mixed __insert (static) + __child (reactive)
  children hydrate correctly with cursor alignment and reactive updates
- Add edge case tests: mixed children, static utility calls, list/conditional
  regression guards, prop-backed array __list guard
- Success metrics: css()/variants() style refs (class={styles.root}) are the
  primary optimization target — typically 5-10 eliminations per component

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(compiler): add changeset and update design doc status [#1006]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(config): deduplicate wagmi via shamefully-hoist + peer deps

pnpm created two separate wagmi@2.19.5 copies with different peer
dependency hashes. WagmiProvider (from @tagit/config) used one copy
while page components (from @tagit/admin) used another, causing
"useConfig must be used within WagmiProvider" errors on Assets page.

Fix: move wagmi and viem from @tagit/config direct deps to peerDeps
(matching @tagit/contracts pattern) + add .npmrc with shamefully-hoist
so all packages resolve to a single wagmi instance.

feat: Sistema completo de Server-Side Tracking para Meta Ads e Google Ads

Implementação completa de tracking server-side com Meta CAPI e Google Enhanced Conversions.

## Funcionalidades:
- Meta Conversions API (CAPI) totalmente integrada
- Sistema de monitoramento automático de leads
- Captura completa de dados (cookies, UTMs, IP, user agent)
- Deduplicação de eventos com event_id
- Schema SQL com triggers automáticos
- Scripts de teste e monitoramento

## Estrutura:
- config/.env: Credenciais Supabase, Meta, Google
- scripts/meta-capi.js: Envio de eventos para Meta CAPI
- scripts/monitor-leads.js: Monitor automático de conversões
- scripts/test-events.js: Suite de testes
- templates/supabase-schema.sql: Schema da tabela leads
- landing-page/: Exemplos de integração

## Tecnologias:
- Node.js
- Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- Meta Conversions API
- Google Ads Enhanced Conversions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix speech recognition: use correct event listener API

Root cause: expo-speech-recognition v3 does NOT export
addSpeechRecognitionListener. Events must be subscribed via
ExpoSpeechRecognitionModule.addListener() (NativeModule API).

Changes:
- Use srModule.addListener() instead of mod.addSpeechRecognitionListener()
- Use expo-speech-recognition's setCategoryIOS() for audio session instead
  of expo-av (avoids conflicts between two modules managing same session)
- Set playAndRecord + defaultToSpeaker + spokenAudio mode
- Remove all expo-av audio session management from TTS and voice hooks
- TTS no longer touches audio session — interview configures it once

commit.message:
fix: switch Stop hook back to command type with exit code 2

Prompt hooks {ok: false} don't grant Claude a new turn (known bug #20221).
Command hooks with exit code 2 + stderr is the reliable mechanism for
blocking Stop and continuing the conversation.

Also fixed: read input from stdin (not $ARGUMENTS), removed wrong
JSON decision format.

commit.message:
Fix auto-range compressor for all color sources (vel/press/vorticity)

Stats compute shader now samples the same source as the display shader
based on a uniform source selector. Bind groups include vel, press, and
curl textures with proper flip variants (8 BG combos). Stats values use
the same normalization divisors as the display shader so the auto-range
works correctly regardless of which color source is selected.

  inline query against loan_officer_assignments table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: whatsfordinner
repository.description: AI-powered weekly meal plans + grocery lists delivered to your inbox. Built with Next.js, Claude AI, and Supabase.

commit.message:
Fix: uid() now generates valid UUIDs required by Supabase

The old uid() returned short random strings like 'k7x9mz2qp' which
Supabase rejected because all ID columns are UUID type. Replaced with
a crypto.getRandomValues()-based RFC 4122 UUID v4 generator.

This fixes 'Failed to create' error when creating a profile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: caltrack
repository.description: CalTrack - Personal Calorie & Macro Tracker PWA

commit.message:
Cathedral v3.5: Icon rail sidebar, pipeline tracker, kanban, deal detail, role-based homes

Complete UX overhaul merging C3 + Pipeline Tracker + CRM into one unified platform.

Navigation:
- Icon rail sidebar (60px collapsed, 240px expanded on hover) replaces topbar+tabbar
- Command palette (Cmd+K) with keyboard navigation and quick actions
- Mobile hamburger button with responsive breakpoints at 768px/1000px/1200px

Pipeline & Deals:
- Pipeline tracker with HTMX filter pills, smart presets (urgent/stale/mine/newest)
- Kanban board with stage columns and entity cards
- Deal detail page with blocking issue banners, post-bind checklist, similar deals
- Age coloring system: 0-2d green, 3-5d blue, 6-9d orange, 10d+ red
- Stage pipeline pills with visual progression (New → Reviewing → Quoted → Negotiating → Bound)

Role-Based Workspaces:
- 5 roles: CEO, Underwriter, Program Manager, Operations, Sales
- Action-first home pages with DO FIRST / THEN / AGING sections
- Morning banners with role-specific greeting and key metrics

UX Features:
- Onboarding tour for new users (5 steps, localStorage persistence)
- Inline glossary tooltips for insurance terms (BOR, UW, TPA, etc.)
- Undo toast notifications with 8-second window
- Hover previews on entity cards
- 3 themes (Obsidian/Slate/Pearl) with sidebar-specific tokens

Models:
- UserPreference (sidebar pins, collapsed sections, onboarding state)
- ActivityEvent (team activity feed with event types)
- Added program_manager and sales roles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
